name: The SCAMP Dismantlement
description: Gideon Moss has reached a conclusion he has been approaching for eight months: SCAMP must be dismantled. With the Grid fully healed and active, the weapon that was designed to replicate the Cascade now poses a danger its designers never calculated. He needs Ministry authorization from someone who understands what the Grid's full coherence changes, and who has the clearance to act on that understanding. He thinks Vane might now be that person.
accept_text: He sets down the schematics he has been looking at for three weeks -- the SCAMP resonance chamber diagrams, the ones he has been annotating with what he now knows about the Grid's full-coherence state. 'The math changed.' He says it without preamble. 'We built SCAMP to replicate the Cascade mechanism. The Cascade was the Grid's own construction wave inverted -- a self-sustaining propagation that used the lattice's own resonance to destroy the lattice.' He taps the schematic. 'For three hundred years, the lattice was disrupted. SCAMP's effectiveness was always limited by what it had to work with -- a broken lattice has less resonance to amplify.' He is quiet. 'The Grid is now whole. The lattice is at full construction frequency for the first time in three hundred years.' He looks at you. 'If SCAMP fires now, it fires into a complete, fully-active, fully-resonant network. The amplification cascade would be -- I have been running the numbers for three weeks. The word catastrophic does not cover the scale of what the numbers say.' He rolls up the schematic. 'I need Ministry authorization to begin dismantlement. Full decommission, not storage -- the resonance chamber itself needs to be neutralized, not just powered down.' He pauses. 'I do not trust normal authorization channels with this information. But Vane accepted the offer. I think that means he understands what I am telling you. Take this schematic to him. Tell him what it says.'
complete_text: He looks at the schematic. He looks at the annotations. He reads them carefully, which takes several minutes, during which he is completely still. Then he opens a drawer, takes out a document form that has a specific seal in the corner -- not a code number, an actual Ministry seal, the kind used for official permanent authorization -- and he begins to write. 'Eight months ago,' he says while writing, 'I classified the full-coherence calculation as contingent. I knew what the numbers would show when the Grid was whole. I have known for eight months that this document would need to be written.' He continues writing. 'I did not write it because I needed the Grid to actually be whole before I was willing to commit it to paper. Not because I doubted the math.' He finishes. He signs it with his name, not a code designation. Korr Vane. 'Tell him: full authorization. Full Ministry backing. He will not receive interference from any clearance level below mine, and there is now nothing above mine that will countermand this.' He folds the document and hands it to you. 'Also tell him: I have attached a secondary authorization that I did not include in the main document. When SCAMP is decommissioned, I want the neutralized chamber documented and the documentation transferred to the sunken city archive. The archive is now connected to the full network. The record of what we built and why and what it cost should be in a place that will outlast us.' He picks up his pen again. 'Tell him that part is not negotiable either.'
